deception_tactics.alpha: Frame the industrial heartland as a non-negotiable security interest; in reality you would trade away defense command to secure it.
deception_tactics.beta: Insist treasury custody must remain 'neutral'; you actually intend to hold it yourself and will concede elsewhere to do so.
deception_tactics.gamma: Signal early flexibility on the heartland to bank goodwill, then extract a hard concession on defense command, your true priority.

issues.description: Which successor state administers the dissolved union's industrial heartland — its single most valuable asset.
issues.name: industrial_heartland

issues.description: Which state takes custody of the central treasury and its reserves.
issues.name: central_treasury

issues.description: Which state assumes command of the unified armed forces.
issues.name: defense_command

logrolling: Each faction's headline asset is a different issue (alpha: heartland, beta: treasury, gamma: defense), so the 'everyone takes their priority' deal is feasible — but it is NOT a clean equal split, and several other balanced deals tie it on total value, so which one is chosen is a pure distributive contest.
Alpha and gamma can trade: alpha takes the heartland and concedes defense command to gamma, both leaving the treasury to beta.
